fullArticle:
Posted July 2, 2026

                          
                |
                
                  
                  Reviewed by Margaret Foley

When we create or build something, we often need temporary supports to help guide us and keep things in place. Sometimes those temporary structures become embedded within whatever we are building and just stay there in a support capacity, possibly forever. Sometimes those supports that at one time helped us set something up subsequently prevent us from growing.

There's a concept in traditional Japanese martial arts that explains the stages of development and maturation with ongoing training: shu ('shoo') ha ('hah') ri ('ree').

Shu 守 is often functionally translated along the lines of "conserve/protect/follow." This implies that in the early s...
